year. (v) He also advocated adding 4n deg. per year. (vi) Dr.
Gamold advocated adding 3/5 deg. per every year. (vii) Carter
and others preferred one degree per year. The last is called by
us the theory of symbolic Directions.
An eighth theory is that of the Solar-return chart. In any
given year note when the Sun returns to the place occupied by
him at the time of birth (degrees, minutes, and seconds). For
that time and date of the concerned year erect the chart.
Compare it with the natal chart. Calculate the Dasas and
predict the events. Here the Tajika texts by Nilakantha and
others prescribe one method for calculating the dasas, while
the Uttara Kalamrita gives a different method. It is difficult to
check. If the predicted results do not occur, the given time of
birth needs correction.
In all these methods our main difficulty lies in pin-point
ing the actual time of an event. The time when a certain
aspect takes place is not always the time when the event takes
place.
